cards.back: A consortium of companies including Google, HTC, Motorola, Samsung, and others that developed the Android operating system.
An open-source mobile operating system developed by the Open Handset Alliance, designed to be highly customizable and extensible.
The Android ecosystem refers to the collection of hardware, software, and services that work together to provide a seamless user experience, including devices, apps, and Google services.

cards.front: What is the Android OS Architecture?
cards.back: The Android OS architecture consists of the kernel, runtime environment, and applications, which work together to manage system resources, execute applications, and provide a user interface.

cards.front: What is the significance of Android Open Source Project (AOSP)?
cards.back: AOSP is a free and open-source implementation of the Android OS, which serves as the foundation for custom ROMs, devices, and applications, and provides a platform for developers to build and test Android apps and devices.
